howItWorks.title: Your garden in 3 simple steps
howItWorks.subtitle: From seed to harvest, we make gardening simple and fun.
howItWorks.step1Desc: Enter the dimensions, soil type, climate zone and sun exposure. It takes less than 2 minutes!
howItWorks.step2Desc: Browse 300+ plants with smart recommendations based on your garden. We tell you what grows best in your space.
howItWorks.step3Desc: View your garden in 3D, follow your care calendar and ask the AI gardener when you need help.

testimonials.title: Loved by 10,000+ Gardeners
testimonials.subtitle: Join a community of gardeners who plan better and harvest more.

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.subtitle: Everything you need to know to get started with your garden.
faq.q1: When should I start my vegetable garden?
faq.a1: The best time to start depends on your climate zone. Generally, you can start seedlings indoors from February-March, and plant outdoors after the last frost (April-May in temperate climates). Our planting calendar automatically adapts the dates to your region.
faq.q2: How much time should I spend gardening each day?
faq.a2: For a small garden (10-20 m²), expect about 15-30 minutes per day for watering, weeding and maintenance. GardenSaas optimizes your time by giving you priority tasks each day.
faq.q3: Does the app work for balconies and small spaces?
faq.a3: Absolutely! GardenSaas supports gardens of all sizes, including balconies and terraces. We recommend the most suitable plants for your available space, with dwarf varieties and aromatic herbs ideal for pot growing.
faq.q4: How does the 3D garden view work?
faq.a4: The 3D view lets you visualize your garden like a video game. You can move your gardener character, see your plants in 3D, and plan the layout of your garden in a fun and intuitive way.
faq.q5: What plants are easiest for a beginner?
faq.a5: We recommend starting with cherry tomatoes, basil, lettuce, radishes and zucchini. These plants are robust, grow quickly and forgive beginner mistakes. Our 'easy difficulty' filter will help you find them.
faq.q6: Does the AI advisor replace a real gardener?
faq.a6: Our AI is a great complement, not a replacement! It analyzes your soil, climate and plants to give personalized advice 24/7. For complex cases, it will point you to specialized resources.
faq.q7: Can I use GardenSaas without internet?
faq.a7: The plant encyclopedia and your garden plan are accessible offline once loaded. Features like real-time weather and the AI advisor require an internet connection.
faq.a8: Companion planting is the art of placing certain species side by side so they help each other. For example, basil protects tomatoes from aphids, and carrots repel the onion fly. GardenSaas automatically shows you good and bad pairings.

location.title: Garden Location
location.description: Where is your garden? This helps us give weather-based watering tips!
location.autoDetect: Auto-detect GPS coordinates from your device
location.locationOptional: Location is optional but recommended for weather-based watering alerts.
location.geoError: Could not detect location. Try entering a city name instead.
location.cityNotFound: City not found. Try a different name.

soil.title: Know Your Earth
soil.description: Different soil, different superpowers! What does your garden have?
soil.clayDesc: Dense & heavy. Holds water well but drains slowly.
soil.sandyDesc: Light & gritty. Drains fast but needs more water.
soil.loamyDesc: The gold standard! Rich, balanced, and fertile.

climate.title: Your Climate Zone
climate.description: Where in the world does your garden live?
climate.tropicalDesc: Hot & humid year-round. Paradise for tropical plants!
climate.subtropicalDesc: Warm with mild winters. Great growing season!
climate.mediterraneanDesc: Hot dry summers, mild wet winters. Classic!
climate.temperateDesc: Four distinct seasons. Most versatile for gardening!
climate.continentalDesc: Hot summers, cold winters. Strong seasonal shifts.
climate.subarcticDesc: Short cool summers, long cold winters. Hardy plants only!

sun.title: Sun Power
sun.description: How much sunshine does your garden get?
sun.fullSunDesc: Your garden is a sun champion! Most veggies will thrive here.
sun.partialShadeDesc: A nice mix! Perfect for leafy greens and herbs.
sun.fullShadeDesc: Shady haven. Great for ferns, lettuce, and mushrooms!
